close_reason: All child tasks are closed, including the tmux cleanup/runtime teardown follow-up verified in 52a4283.
description:
Follow-up work from real-world beadwork usage on another dev machine. Scope this epic to `packages/pi-beadwork-extension`.

Requested outcomes:
- harden the `land` path around rebase/merge failures and retry behavior
- respect configured worker concurrency limits during orchestration
- support a per-delegate model override for one-off runs
- clean up `.pi/beadwork/workers/runtime` artifacts alongside worker/worktree teardown

Recommended execution lanes to avoid merge stomps:
1. Landing lane (shared files/flow; prefer one worker or one serial branch):
   - mechanical rebase failure should resubmit with LLM assistance
   - preserve passed review when final rebase/merge fails and land is retried
   - fix dirty-worktree / `context.md` interaction during landing
2. Independent lane: respect configured concurrent worker limit during run
3. Independent lane: add per-delegate `--model provider/model` override
4. Independent lane: clean up worker runtime state/logs when worktree is removed

Definition of done for implementation tickets:
- add/update tests under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`
- run `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- run `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- run `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
title: Stabilize beadwork landing flow and worker orchestration follow-ups
